1. Introduction

About This Book

This book is the definitive toolkit for musicians, producers, and creative technologists. Inside you’ll find a comprehensive collection of genre prompts, sound design blueprints, and advanced experimental templates: from classic hip-hop and rock to cybernetic fusion, AI-driven lyric engines, and chaos music theory.

How to Use These Prompts

  • Producers: Start or hybridize tracks using these templates.

  • Songwriters: Explore fresh lyric themes and arrangement ideas, or harness AI-assisted mutation strategies.

  • Live Acts: Apply performance layouts for improvisation and spontaneous genre-blending.

  • Engineers: Reference mixing, panning, and FX chains.

  • AI/Tech: Implement code and structures for generative song systems.

2. AI, Songwriting, and Performance

Building an AI-Driven Music System

  • Develop Neural Lyric Mutation Engine

  • Connect MIDI to lyric generation

  • Create live performance setups

  • Build interactive performer interfaces

  • Prototype real-time lyric mutation

The Neural Lyric Mutation Engine

  • BPM-synced word/phrase alteration

  • Polyrhythmic flow and mood detection

  • Real-time phonetic morphing, Markov chains

  • Dynamic chorus harmonies

  • Beat-triggered lyric expansion or fragmentation

Example Project: "Digital Moonshine Prophecy" – AI morphs verses/choruses in real time, responding to tempo and mood.

3. Genre Prompts & Sound Design

Hip-Hop, Rap & Fusion Styles

  • Cloud Rap: Dreamy pads, soft 808s, wide stereo, BPM: 80–85.

  • Alternative Hip-Hop: Experimental synths, off-kilter drums, BPM: 92.

  • Trap: Dark 140 BPM, eerie bells, deep 808s, crisp snares.

  • Drill: Menacing 144 BPM, gliding 808s, orchestral stabs.

  • Grime: Aggressive, minimal, BPM: 140.

  • Horrorcore: Eerie melodies, sampled screams, BPM: 70.

  • Trap Soul: Soulful, deep 808s, smooth, BPM: 90.

  • Global/Underground Fusion: Grime Drill (UK+Drill), K-Hip-Hop, Vaporwave Rap.

Each beat prompt provides specific instructions on instrumentation, sound design, and production techniques to achieve the desired style. The guide explores detailed example prompts for different rap beat subgenres, offering inspiration and guidance for creating unique and innovative music tracks.

Rock & Metal Subgenres

  • Hard Rock: Crunchy guitars, punchy drums.

  • Grunge: Raw, overdriven, moody.

  • Shoegaze: Layered, reverbed, ethereal.

  • Stoner/Doom/Industrial: Each with detailed instrumentation, mixing, and effects.

Country, Americana & Experimental Fusions

  • Outlaw: Twangy guitars, shuffled drums.

  • Bluegrass: Fast acoustic, upright bass, harmonies.

  • Cinematic/Swamp/Lo-Fi/Americana: Strings, slide guitar, analog warmth.

  • Experimental: Drone Country, Cyber-Western, Quantum Country, Bioacoustic.

4. Iconic Artist, Label, & Scene Blueprints

Signature Sound Templates

  • OutKast, Sleep Token, Upchurch, Lil Peep, Nirvana, Rage Against the Machine: Instrumentation, arrangement, production mapped for each.

Label Sounds

  • No Limit: Booming 808s, cinematic synths, chant hooks.

  • Cash Money: New Orleans bounce, bright leads.

  • Death Row: G-Funk and West Coast.

  • Others: Def Jam, Roc-A-Fella, Bad Boy—each with signature drums, bass, and mixing.

5. Advanced Arrangement & Production Techniques

  • Layering, panning, and wide mixes

  • Compression/distortion for analog warmth or chaos

  • Reverb/delay: from cavernous to granular

  • Real-time AI lyric mutation: BPM sync, semantic expansion, polyrhythm

  • Quantum, fractal, and chaos sound design

6. Sample Packs, Chopped Scripts & Performance Templates

Southern Gothic & Horrorcore: Memphis soul/gospel samples, horror film dialogue, preacher sermon FX. Vocal hooks and sermon scripts for intros, FX suggestions, and tips for resampling, pitch/fx layering in DAW or live performance bring each genre to life.

7. Appendices

Prompt Index

Organized by genre with BPM, key arrangement, and sound notes for each.

AI Lyric Mutation: Algorithms & Implementation

Code/logic for generative lyric mutation, Markov/entropy engines.

Resources & Further Reading

Songwriting, production, AI music, experimental sound design.

Featured Seductive & Experimental Prompts

  • 2. “PUPPET STRINGS” — YOU ARE NOT IN CONTROL

  • Inspiration: Kanye’s “Black Skinhead”, Fever Ray, Gesaffelstein, Björk

  • Industrial-electronic, primal drums, metallic clangs, pulsing bass, flickering synths, layered inhuman vocals. Lyrics: "Let go… I pull your strings…"

  • 3. “DOMINION” — THE SOUND OF BEING CLAIMED

  • Inspiration: Depeche Mode, Nine Inch Nails, Rosalia, Burial

  • Slow, ritualistic, regal rhythm, seductive vocal delivery, lyrics of devotion and domination.

  • 4. “PARASITE” — LET ME FEED ON YOUR DESIRE

  • Inspiration: Massive Attack, Arca, FKA Twigs, The Weeknd

  • Seductive trip-hop, warped samples, intimate vocals, lyrics: "Let me inside… I want to taste…"

  • 5. “OBLIVION TOUCH” — THE FINAL DESCENT

  • Inspiration: Tool, Crosses, Björk, Sade

  • Doom-laced, slow, psychedelic—hypnotic beats, soft vulnerable vocals, lyrics on surrender and oblivion.

How To Use: Load prompts into Suno, Udio, Riffusion, or DAW. Adjust as needed for BPM, style, and mood. Layer atmospheric FX, breaths, moans, ritual drones for extra effect.

Lyric & Production Masterclass

  • Blues Tale: Gritty, storytelling lyrics; wailing guitar, slide, harmonica, live ambience.

  • Lost in the Storm: Emotional, cinematic arrangement; atmospheric guitars, layered harmonies.

Vocal Guidelines: Verses: Deep, storytelling, Southern drawl; Chorus: Raw, anthemic, gospel harmonies; Bridge: Whispered, close-mic’d, vulnerable.

Mixing Tips: Tape warmth, parallel excitement, stereo widening, minimal blues compression.

AI Tools & Portfolio Integration

  • Suno: Full tracks from text prompts

  • Udio: Clips/remix/tag-based

  • Riffusion: Spectrogram-based, unique samples

  • Portfolio steps: Generate, download, upload, add metadata

User Testing & Feedback

  • A/B, usability, surveys, heatmaps

  • Define goals, recruit testers, analyze, iterate

Closing & Invitation

This living prompt book is engineered for growth. Combine, remix, and create—request custom prompts for any style or artist. The only rule: break them all.

Appendix

  • Full prompt list

  • Lyric examples

  • Mixing cheat sheet

  • AI tool quickstart

  • User testing template

Award-Winning Music Prompt Book by TheManWhoSoldTheWorld (2025)

Crafted for musicians, producers, and creators everywhere.

It includes various beat prompts for different rap subgenres like cloud rap, alternative hip-hop, experimental rap, Latin drill, mumble trap, post-trap, horrorcore, grime, hardcore hip-hop, ambient rap, trap soul, synthwave rap, and more. Each beat prompt provides specific instructions on instrumentation, sound design, and production techniques to achieve the desired style. The document also explores detailed example prompts for different rap beat subgenres, offering inspiration and guidance for creating unique and innovative music tracks.

Beat prompts

Wednesday, January 29, 2025

3:40 PM

 

🌌 Alternative & Avant-Garde Styles

Cloud Rap

"Design a soft, dreamy cloud rap beat at 85 BPM with airy, distant synth pads and reverb-heavy 808s. Use a smooth vintage piano as a lead melody, and place hi-hats wide for spaciousness. Layer in a subtle reverb-drenched snare, panned slightly off-center to enhance the floating feeling. The 808 bass should be prominent, yet not overpowering, with a warmth that adds depth without muddling the mix. Add ambient vocal chops that sit in the background, panned left/right, for a distant, ghostly vibe."

 

"Craft a minimalistic cloud rap beat with a Lo-Fi drum kit, using soft, analog drum sounds with a slight distortion for warmth. The kick and snare should be subtle but punchy, with the snare panned slightly to the left to create space. Layer a soft synth pad with a slow, melodic lead that carries the track’s main hook, panning it wide left and right for an airy, expansive feel. Use a reverb-heavy vocal sample as a melodic backdrop, panned to the far right for a dreamlike ambiance."

 

"Create a futuristic cloud rap beat with soft, ambient synths that roll into melodic chimes. The 808 bass should be deep and round, but mellow enough to stay in the background. The snare should be distant and panned slightly left to add a sense of movement without taking over the space. Keep the hi-hats at a light off-center panning, almost feeling like a subtle breeze. Add a reverb-heavy lead with vocals that echo and blend into the track, placed gently in the center to float between the sounds."

 

Alternative Hip-Hop

"Produce a 92 BPM beat with off-kilter drum breaks from an MPC 2000XL, layered with ambient synths and chopped vocal samples. The kick drum should be punchy and distorted, while the snare should have a lot of presence, panned slightly to the right for separation. The hi-hats should be light and dynamic, placed hard left, to make the mix feel spacious. Add a bassline from a Moog Sub 37 for that thick analog warmth, with a subtle wobble that stands out in the center but doesn’t overpower the melody."

 

"Build an alternative hip-hop beat with guitar loops, light trap percussion, and spacey melodies. The kick drum should have punch, but with an organic tone, while the snare hits are soft, with a bit of reverb to create space. Layer in a subtle bassline from a Korg MS-20 synth, panned center, to give the track grounding. Use vocal chops that are more experimental and panned slightly left to create movement, with pads that slowly evolve over time, creating an ambient atmosphere throughout."

 

"Use a Korg MS-20 for experimental synths and 808 drums for an alternative vibe. The snare drum should be crisp with a sharp slap, panned slightly right to give a sense of motion, while hi-hats are far left for contrast. Layer in vocals processed with a bit of auto-tune and reverb, placed slightly off-center to create an emotional, somewhat disjointed feel. Add unconventional percussion sounds—metallic hits, distant claps, or synthetic clicks—panned across the stereo field for a textured, experimental sound."

 

Experimental Rap

"Design a 100 BPM experimental beat with glitchy, distorted snares and randomized percussion. Start with a TR-808 for deep kicks, and add broken-up, fragmented drum hits that sound like they’re being manipulated. The hi-hats should be placed hard right with panned delays to give them a stuttering effect. Incorporate reverse-engineered synths, sweeping pads, and field recordings (like mechanical noises or speech snippets), panned left and right to create a chaotic, unpredictable atmosphere. The vocals should be distorted, with sidechain compression to keep the mix clear yet lively."

 

"Create an experimental rap beat with futuristic, unpredictable sounds from the Elektron Digitakt, layered with reversed samples and chopped-up vocals. The snare drums should be minimal, but heavily distorted for added grit. Keep the kick drum deep and centered, but introduce shifting, glitchy percussion that adds randomness to the rhythm. Vocal samples should be panned wide to create a sense of distance and movement, while the background should have layered static and noise that adds tension without overwhelming the beat."

 

"Construct a 90 BPM track using broken synths, distorted percussion, and scratched vinyl sounds. Keep the 808 bass centered and deep, while using filter sweeps on synth lines to create evolving textures. The snare drum should be slightly off-center, and use ambience that fluctuates in the background. Add reverse effects on the vocals, making them appear to come from another dimension, panned far left/right to push the track further into experimental territory. The final mix should feel nonlinear and unconventional."

 

💥 Trap & Hybrid Styles

Latin Drill

“Design a 140 BPM Latin drill beat with reggaeton percussion (congas, shakers), distorted 808s, and a haunting synth lead. Layer in Spanish vocal samples, panned wide for a dark yet rhythmic atmosphere. The kick should be heavy but slightly distorted, with snare drums that snap, panned slightly right to add separation. Add bright, melodic synths on top, panned left, to provide contrast with the dark atmosphere, and use shuffling percussive fills to enhance the groove.”

 

“Create a drill beat with punchy 808s from a TR-808 and sharp snares. Layer tropical percussion (bongos, timbales), and pan them to the right for extra rhythm and energy. Introduce a creepy, atmospheric synth on the left for an eerie vibe. Keep the vocal samples panned centered, but with a tremolo effect that gives them a hypnotic feel. Add in a distant, echoing snare that complements the punchy drums for extra tension.”

 

“At 140 BPM, build a beat with hard 808s, dark synth melodies, and reggaeton-style percussion loops. Use Spanish vocal chops and pan them wide, with heavy reverb to give it a sinister vibe. The bassline should be deep and aggressive, while snare rolls should be fast-paced and panned slightly left, to balance the forward motion with ambient background layers that feel atmospheric yet driving.”

 

Mumble Trap

"Craft a 130 BPM beat with mellow 808s, soft snares, and ambient synths. Place hi-hats on the left for spaciousness, and keep vocals in the center with a bit of reverb to allow for floaty mumble flows. The kick should be deep but restrained, with snare rolls that offer just enough snap. Use soft synth pads in the background, panned left and right, to add texture without dominating the mix."

 

"Create a laid-back mumble trap beat with minimalist 808 kicks and dreamy synth pads. The vocals should be laid-back and processed with heavy auto-tune and reverb for that floating, ethereal sound. Place hi-hats hard left, and keep the snare light but crisp. Add a background drone pad that sits in the center, weaving in and out of the mix with a subtle filter modulation for an evolving texture."

 

"Design a 130 BPM mumble trap beat with subtle percussion and reverb-heavy synths. The 808 bass should dominate center, while the hi-hats and vocals should be placed in opposing stereo channels for contrast. Add melodic guitar loops, panned left/right, that enhance the chilled vibe. The vocals should be front-and-center but with a low pass filter to create an underwater, distant effect, enhancing the relaxed atmosphere."

 

Post-Trap

"Design a 95 BPM post-trap beat with deep, soft 808s and ambient synths. Add soft drums from a TR-909, with panned atmospheric pads on the left and whispered vocals layered on the right. Keep the bassline centered but filtered, allowing it to stay prominent without becoming too overpowering. Add subtle high-passed percussion for extra texture, while delayed vocals can add a dreamy, melancholic element that perfectly complements the track."

 

"Craft a post-trap beat with melancholic piano chords, smooth 808 bass, and slow snares. Keep the hi-hats light and placed to the right, and use subtle synth melodies for a reflective, modern vibe. The vocals should be airy, with auto-tune processing that adds depth. Add delayed synth layers that evolve throughout the track, panned slightly left, creating an ethereal atmosphere of contemplation."

 

"Create a post-trap vibe with soft 808s, ambient pads, and subtle drums. Add a synth lead panned far left, while keeping the vocals in the center with a smooth, emotional delivery. The background should evolve with gentle reverb tails and subtle delay effects to create a nostalgic, atmospheric feeling. Let the bass move freely while keeping the higher frequencies light to allow the overall mix to feel spacious and open."

 

 

cont

Wednesday, January 29, 2025

3:45 PM

 

Here are detailed example prompts for different rap beat subgenres:

 

🔥 Trap Beat Prompt

"A dark and hard-hitting trap beat at 140 BPM, featuring deep, distorted 808 basslines that glide between notes. The drums hit with a punchy kick, crisp snare, and fast hi-hat rolls with triplets. The melody consists of eerie bell plucks and ambient synth pads drenched in reverb, creating a moody, atmospheric feel. Occasional risers, reversed cymbals, and vocal chops add depth, while a subtle tape-stop effect introduces smooth transitions. The overall mix is clean yet aggressive, perfect for a modern rap anthem."

 

🎵 Inspiration: Future, Metro Boomin, 21 Savage, Travis Scott

 

🎤 Boom-Bap Beat Prompt

"A classic East Coast boom-bap beat at 92 BPM, built around a dusty jazz piano sample layered with warm vinyl crackle. The drums are punchy and raw, featuring a fat, knocking kick, snappy snare, and loose swing hi-hats for that golden-era groove. A deep upright bass loop anchors the track, adding a soulful foundation. Subtle horn stabs and vintage vocal chops bring a nostalgic energy. The overall mix has a slightly lo-fi feel, with analog warmth and subtle tape saturation for authenticity."

 

🎵 Inspiration: J. Cole, Nas, Wu-Tang Clan, MF DOOM

 

🔥 Drill Beat Prompt

"A dark and aggressive UK/NY drill beat at 144 BPM, featuring heavy 808 basslines that glide menacingly with distorted texture. The drum pattern has a signature drill bounce with skippy hi-hats, rolling snares, and sliding percussion. The melody consists of haunting orchestral strings and deep choir pads, adding an ominous cinematic vibe. Subtle gunshot FX and deep reverb on the snare create a raw, street feel. The overall mix is clean but hard-hitting, emphasizing the low-end for maximum impact."

 

🎵 Inspiration: Pop Smoke, Chief Keef, Central Cee, Fivio Foreign

 

🌴 West Coast G-Funk Beat Prompt

"A smooth, laid-back West Coast beat at 100 BPM, featuring funky basslines, lush electric piano chords, and a talkbox synth lead. The drums have a bouncy groove with crisp claps, live hi-hats, and deep kicks. Layered over the mix are wah-wah guitar licks and vintage synth stabs, giving it a classic G-funk feel. The beat is mixed with warm analog saturation, enhancing its retro yet timeless bounce. Perfect for cruising with the top down under the Cali sun."

 

🎵 Inspiration: Dr. Dre, Snoop Dogg, Nate Dogg, DJ Quik

 

☁️ Lo-Fi Hip-Hop Beat Prompt

"A mellow and nostalgic lo-fi hip-hop beat at 80 BPM, featuring a smooth Rhodes piano loop layered with subtle vinyl crackle for warmth. The drums are soft and laid-back, with a deep but muted kick, airy snare, and lazy swing hi-hats. A warm, jazzy bassline sits low in the mix, complementing the dreamy atmosphere. Gentle rain sounds and reversed vocal chops fade in and out, adding texture and depth. The entire beat is drenched in tape saturation and a slight low-pass filter, giving it a cozy, vintage vibe."

 

🎵 Inspiration: J Dilla, Nujabes, Lofi Girl, Chillhop

 

🕶️ Memphis Phonk Beat Prompt

"A dark and gritty Memphis phonk beat at 145 BPM, built around a heavily distorted 808 bass and chopped-up, eerie soul samples. The drums hit hard with crunchy snares, fast hi-hat patterns, and rolling cowbells that add a hypnotic rhythm. Vocal samples from vintage Memphis rap tapes echo throughout, drenched in reverb and pitch-shifted for that raw underground feel. The beat is mixed with aggressive compression and slight tape hiss for an authentic, dirty sound."

 

🎵 Inspiration: DJ Screw, Three 6 Mafia, $uicideboy$, SpaceGhostPurrp

 

🏆 Southern Crunk Beat Prompt

"An energetic Southern crunk beat at 150 BPM, featuring a booming 808 kick, sharp claps, and a rapid hi-hat pattern. The melody is driven by eerie brass stabs and aggressive synth leads, creating a hype club atmosphere. Chant-like vocal adlibs (“Yeah!” “Okay!”) are sprinkled throughout, adding a rowdy feel. Deep reverb and delay effects create massive transitions, while the bass shakes the mix with high-energy distortion. Perfect for getting hyped in the club or at a party."

 

🎵 Inspiration: Lil Jon, Ying Yang Twins, Three 6 Mafia

 

🌧️ Lil Peep-Style Emo Rap Beat Prompt

"A melancholic emo rap beat at 82 BPM, blending moody guitar melodies with deep, atmospheric textures. The main melody features a reverbed electric guitar loop, playing a sorrowful, arpeggiated chord progression with a slightly detuned, lo-fi feel. A warm, pulsating 808 bassline adds emotional weight, gliding smoothly under the melody. The drums are trap-inspired but laid-back, featuring a deep, punchy kick, crisp yet soft snares, and airy hi-hats with subtle rolls. In the background, dreamy pad synths and distant vocal chops add a hazy, nostalgic texture. Light vinyl crackle and distant rain sounds enhance the emotional depth. The overall mix is slightly washed-out with reverb and tape saturation, giving it a raw and intimate feel, perfect for heartfelt lyrics and melodic flows."

 

🎵 Inspiration: Lil Peep, XXXTentacion, Juice WRLD, Wifisfuneral

 

💔 Lil Peep-Style Emo Rap Beat – Acoustic & Dark Variations

🎸 Acoustic Emo Rap Beat Prompt (Sad & Melodic)

"A deeply emotional emo rap beat at 78 BPM, featuring a raw, fingerpicked acoustic guitar loop drenched in subtle reverb and tape warmth. The guitar plays a melancholic chord progression, evoking nostalgia and heartbreak. The drums are soft and organic, with a slightly muted 808 kick, airy snares, and loose hi-hats for a laid-back groove. A deep, rumbling sub-bass adds warmth without overpowering the mix. Dreamy vocal chops with a ghostly, ethereal feel drift in the background, layered with soft synth pads for extra emotional depth. The mix is intentionally raw, with slight distortion and a vintage cassette-like texture, creating an intimate, lo-fi aesthetic perfect for personal, soul-baring lyrics."

 

🎵 Inspiration: Lil Peep – Star Shopping, XXXTentacion – Jocelyn Flores

 

🖤 Dark & Grungy Emo Trap Beat Prompt (Gritty & Haunting)

"A dark and moody emo trap beat at 85 BPM, built around a gritty, overdriven electric guitar riff with heavy reverb and slight distortion, creating a washed-out, underground feel. The 808 bass is deep and aggressive, gliding between notes for a wavy, unsettling energy. The drum pattern is hard-hitting yet spacious, featuring a punchy kick, crisp snare with a slight crunch, and open hi-hats that give a raw, punk-inspired bounce. Layered in the background are ghostly reversed synth pads and eerie, chopped-up vocal samples, drenched in reverb and delay to create a haunting atmosphere. The entire mix has a hazy, DIY aesthetic, with a touch of analog saturation for that underground SoundCloud grunge-rap sound."

 

🎵 Inspiration: Lil Peep – Crybaby, Ghostemane – Mercury, $uicideboy$

#### **🔥 Trap Beat**

*"A dark 140 BPM trap beat with eerie bell plucks, deep gliding 808s, crisp snares, and fast hi-hat rolls. Reverb-heavy pads and vocal chops add a moody, atmospheric feel."*

 

#### **🎤 Boom-Bap Beat**

*"A nostalgic 92 BPM boom-bap beat with dusty jazz piano, warm vinyl crackle, punchy kicks, crisp snares, and groovy basslines. Lo-fi textures add a vintage golden-era feel."*

 

#### **🔥 Drill Beat**

*"A menacing 144 BPM drill beat with gliding 808s, skippy hi-hats, rolling snares, and eerie orchestral strings. Reverb-heavy vocal chops and FX create an aggressive, cinematic sound."*

 

#### **🌴 West Coast G-Funk Beat**

*"A bouncy 100 BPM West Coast beat with funky basslines, lush piano chords, talkbox synths, crisp claps, and groovy drums. Wah-wah guitars and synth stabs add a classic G-funk feel."*

 

#### **☁️ Lo-Fi Hip-Hop Beat**

#### **🕶️ Memphis Phonk Beat**

#### **🎸 Acoustic Emo Rap Beat**

*"A sad 78 BPM emo rap beat with raw acoustic guitar, soft 808s, airy snares, and lo-fi vocal chops. Dreamy pads and tape warmth create an intimate, nostalgic vibe."*

 

#### **🖤 Dark & Grungy Emo Trap Beat**

#### **📼 Lo-Fi Cassette Emo Rap Beat**

🎶 Classic & Underground Styles

Boom-Bap

"A 90s-inspired boom-bap beat at 94 BPM with soulful jazz piano loops, deep drum breaks, and snappy snares. Warm vinyl crackle and subtle horns give it a golden-era vibe for conscious lyricism."

 

Lo-Fi Hip-Hop

"A chill 80 BPM lo-fi beat with mellow Rhodes piano, light drums, soft bass, and gentle vinyl crackle. Rain sounds and a warm tape saturation effect add a cozy, nostalgic atmosphere."

 

Jazz Rap

"A smooth jazz rap beat at 95 BPM with swinging drum breaks, jazzy upright bass, and soulful brass loops. Light piano fills and subtle scratches create a laid-back vibe for lyrical flow."

 

Conscious Hip-Hop

"A 90 BPM conscious hip-hop beat with a soulful piano loop, jazzy basslines, crisp snare, and laid-back hi-hats. Ambient pads and smooth keys create a reflective atmosphere for deep lyricism."

 

Underground Hip-Hop

"An experimental 88 BPM underground hip-hop beat with gritty drum samples, chopped-up vocal snippets, and atmospheric synths. Raw, distorted bass and analog textures give it a rough, underground edge."

 

🔥 Modern & Mainstream Styles

Trap

"A hard-hitting 140 BPM trap beat with sliding 808 bass, crisp snare, fast hi-hats, and dark synth melodies. Vocal chops and ambient pads add mood, while a glitchy drop intensifies the energy."

 

Drill

"A dark 144 BPM drill beat with distorted 808s, skippy hi-hats, aggressive snares, and eerie synth strings. Reverb-heavy vocal chops and hard-hitting FX bring a menacing, cinematic atmosphere."

 

Mumble Rap

"A bouncy 130 BPM mumble rap beat with melodic 808s, sparse kicks, airy snares, and soft hi-hats. Dreamy synths and reverb-drenched vocal chops create a hypnotic, laid-back flow."

 

Melodic Rap

"A smooth 90 BPM melodic rap beat with piano chords, dreamy synths, and a rolling 808 bassline. Soft, airy drums and layered vocal harmonies create an emotional, atmospheric vibe."

 

Rage Beats / Hyperpop Rap

"An energetic 160 BPM hyperpop rap beat with distorted bass, glitchy synths, and hyper-processed drums. Fast tempo, pitched vocals, and experimental sound design give it an electric, futuristic vibe."

 

🌊 Regional & Cultural Styles

West Coast G-Funk

"A laid-back 100 BPM G-funk beat with funky basslines, smooth synths, talkbox melodies, and snappy snares. Deep kicks and ambient pads set the mood for a chill West Coast vibe."

 

Southern Crunk

"A high-energy 150 BPM crunk beat with booming 808s, rapid hi-hats, sharp claps, and chant-like vocal adlibs. Brass stabs and vocal drops create an aggressive, hype atmosphere for the club."

 

Memphis Phonk

"A gritty 145 BPM Memphis phonk beat with distorted 808s, chopped soul samples, eerie vocal clips, and rolling percussion. Vinyl crackle and heavy reverb give it a raw underground edge."

 

Chopped & Screwed

"A slowed-down 70 BPM beat with deep 808s, sparse drums, and a hazy, syrupy atmosphere. Chopped vocal samples and warped melodies create a smooth, hypnotic vibe with a Texas-influenced sound."

 

East Coast Hardcore

"A 95 BPM hardcore East Coast beat with aggressive drum breaks, gritty synth stabs, and dark piano loops. Raw bass and punchy snares create a hard-hitting, underground sound for tough lyricism."

 

🎸 Experimental & Fusion Styles

Emo Rap

"A moody 80 BPM emo rap beat with emotional guitar loops, soft 808s, and atmospheric pads. Light snares, airy hi-hats, and ethereal vocal chops create a nostalgic, introspective vibe."

 

Punk Rap

"A 140 BPM punk rap beat with distorted electric guitars, fast punk drums, and aggressive basslines. Raw, unfiltered energy with quick snare hits and vocal adlibs for a rebellious, high-energy sound."

 

Industrial Rap

"A 100 BPM industrial rap beat with harsh distorted bass, mechanical percussion, and experimental synths. Dark, glitchy textures and deep, booming kicks create an abrasive, dystopian feel."

 

Afro-Trap / Afrobeat Rap

"A rhythmic 110 BPM Afro-trap beat with lively percussion, infectious synth melodies, and deep 808s. Afrobeat rhythms and chant-like vocal patterns create a vibrant, danceable vibe."

 

Latin Trap

"A 90 BPM Latin trap beat with reggaeton-inspired drums, smooth 808 bass, and tropical melodies. Spicy brass stabs and Spanish vocal adlibs create a warm, celebratory vibe for smooth flows."

 

 

cont

Wednesday, January 29, 2025

3:43 PM

 

Alternative & Avant-Garde Styles

Cloud Rap – Dreamy, ethereal beats with soft, ambient melodies, often paired with spacey, abstract lyrics. (e.g., A$AP Rocky, Yung Lean, Lil B)

 

Alternative Hip-Hop – A fusion of hip-hop with other genres like rock, electronic, and jazz, offering experimental production and abstract lyricism. (e.g., Kid Cudi, Earl Sweatshirt, Run The Jewels)

Experimental Rap – A genre that pushes boundaries with unpredictable flows, odd production choices, and unique structures. (e.g., Death Grips, Flying Lotus, JPEGMAFIA)

 

💥 Trap & Hybrid Styles

Latin Drill – A fusion of drill music with Latin rhythms and Spanish-language lyrics, influenced by both UK drill and reggaeton. (e.g., Jhay Cortez, J Balvin)

Mumble Trap – A substyle of trap where the emphasis is more on the energy and vibe than clear, understandable lyrics. (e.g., Lil Yachty, Smokepurpp)

Post-Trap – A more experimental, atmospheric twist on traditional trap, often featuring melancholic or ambient sounds. (e.g., Travis Scott, Lil Uzi Vert)

 

🌪️ Hardcore & Aggressive Styles

Horrorcore – Dark, violent, and often supernatural lyrical content with gritty, eerie production. (e.g., Insane Clown Posse, Tech N9ne, Necro)

Grime – A fast, aggressive UK rap style with rapid-fire delivery and electronic, often grimy beats. (e.g., Skepta, Stormzy, Dizzee Rascal)

Hardcore Hip-Hop – Aggressive and intense delivery over hard, beat-driven production, often with heavy use of street slang and tough subject matter. (e.g., Onyx, DMX, M.O.P.)

 

✨ Experimental & Fusion Styles

Ambient Rap – Focuses on atmospheric, ambient sounds paired with introspective, often slow-moving flows and minimal beats. (e.g., Bones, Night Lovell, XXXTentacion)

Trap Soul – Combines trap beats with soulful melodies and R&B-inspired vocals, often dealing with love and emotional themes. (e.g., Bryson Tiller, 6lack, PARTYNEXTDOOR)

Synthwave Rap – Blends 80s synthwave and electronic beats with rap delivery, giving a retro-futuristic feel. (e.g., Gunna, Travis Scott, Lil Uzi Vert)

 

🌍 Global Styles

Grime Drill – A fusion of UK grime with drill’s dark, aggressive energy, often with regional slang and fast flows. (e.g., Headie One, Digga D, Unknown T)

K-Hip-Hop – Korean hip-hop, with influences from both local Korean music and Western rap styles. (e.g., Jay Park, DPR LIVE, Zico)

French Rap – Rap originating in France, influenced by both global and French culture, with varied styles from boom-bap to trap. (e.g., Booba, Niska, PNL)

 

🖤 Underground & Soundcloud Styles

SoundCloud Rap – Lo-fi, DIY rap often with distorted, minimalist beats and a strong underground ethos. (e.g., Lil Peep, XXXTentacion, Lil Skies)

Vaporwave Rap – A unique combination of vaporwave aesthetics (nostalgic 80s/90s visuals, slow tempos) with rap vocals. (e.g., Bladee, Lil B, Yung Lean)

Screamo Rap – A fusion of rap and screamo/emo elements, often with raw and emotional vocal delivery. (e.g., Scarlxrd, Ghostemane, Lil Darkie)

 

🎧 Future & Experimental Rap

Post-Punk Rap – Combines elements of post-punk rock and rap, with abrasive, edgy beats and introspective lyricism. (e.g., JPEGMAFIA, Yves Tumor, Kid Cudi)

Glitch Rap – Features chopped-up, glitchy beats, often with experimental production and abstract lyrics. (e.g., Clipping., Aesop Rock, Death Grips)

✨ Experimental & Fusion Styles

Ambient Rap

Synthwave Rap

🌍 Global Styles

Grime Drill

French Rap

🖤 Underground & Soundcloud Styles

SoundCloud Rap

"A raw 80 BPM SoundCloud rap beat with distorted 808s, lo-fi synths, and airy hi-hats. Sparse, atmospheric elements and reverb-heavy vocals create a melancholic, DIY aesthetic perfect for underground flows."

 

Vaporwave Rap

"A slow 70 BPM vaporwave rap beat with dreamy synths, retro-futuristic melodies, and soft 808s. Vinyl crackle, smooth pads, and nostalgic elements give it a mellow, atmospheric vibe."

 

Screamo Rap

"A 140 BPM screamo rap beat with heavy guitar distortion, screeching vocals, and intense drums. Fast-paced percussion and aggressive 808s create an abrasive, emotional atmosphere for raw lyricism."

 

🎧 Future & Experimental Rap

Post-Punk Rap

"A 95 BPM post-punk rap beat with edgy, distorted guitar riffs, live drum breaks, and experimental synths. Reverb-heavy, chaotic rhythms create a rebellious, underground atmosphere for emotional lyrics."

 

Glitch Rap

"A 90 BPM glitch rap beat with chopped-up, glitchy synths, distorted vocal samples, and unpredictable percussion. Nonlinear rhythms and experimental sound design create an unconventional, jarring feel."

 

 

 

Wednesday, May 28, 2025

1:27 AM

 

Here’s a 🔥 curated Tracklist of Sample Sources for Evergoing Song, targeting that Three 6 Mafia-style Memphis horrorcore x Southern Gothic vibe:

 

🎙️ Memphis Soul & Southern Vinyl Sample Crates

Use for: looped melodies, ambient intros, and soulful breakdowns.

Source Sample Type Notable Tracks / Artists
📀 Hi Records (Memphis, 1970s) Warm soul loops, brass, falsetto vocals Al Green – "Simply Beautiful", Ann Peebles – "I Can't Stand the Rain"
📀 Stax Records (Memphis, 1960s-70s) Gritty guitar riffs, church organs, rhythm sections Booker T. & the M.G.'s – "Melting Pot", Isaac Hayes – "Hung Up on My Baby"
📀 Goldwax Records Deep gospel and Southern melancholy James Carr – "Dark End of the Street", O.V. Wright – "A Nickel and a Nail"
📼 Field Recordings of Memphis Preachers Preaching loops, gritty sermons, distorted shouts Public domain sermon tapes (Lo-fi AM radio style)
📼 Obscure Soul/Gospel 45s Warm chord progressions, raw harmonies Dig through Gospel compilations like “Fire in My Bones”

 

🎥 Horror & VHS Film Sample Sources

Use for: intros, transitions, outro dialogue, and atmosphere.

Source Sample Type Notable Clips
🎞️ Lucio Fulci Horror Films Creepy dialogue, B-movie screams, heavy ambience The Beyond (1981), City of the Living Dead (1980)
🎞️ 1970s Exploitation / Grindhouse Flicks Warped morality rants, fear quotes I Spit on Your Grave, Maniac (1980)
🎞️ Early Christian Doomsday Tapes Preacher warnings, VHS hiss, tape degradation The Burning Hell (1974), A Thief in the Night series
🎞️ Public Domain Horror Atmospheric organ, thunder, low-budget narration Night of the Living Dead (1968), Carnival of Souls (1962)
📼 Haunted House Sound Effects Tapes Chains, whispers, screams, creaks Often found at thrift shops labeled as “Halloween FX”

 

🧪 Pro Sampling Tips:

  • Use a SP-404 or Koala Sampler VST to chop and degrade loops

  • Resample at 11kHz or lower with a bitcrusher to match 90s Memphis tape quality

  • Layer vinyl crackle and cassette hiss across transitions

  • Always pitch +2/-3 semitones and add reverb tail for shadowy feel

  • 🔥 Drop a sample pack of royalty-free sounds in this style?

  • 📼 Write chopped sample intro scripts using horror preacher lines?

  • 🧠 Build a MIDI layout with chopped vocal sample triggers and pads?

Wednesday, May 28, 2025

1:29 AM

 

Here's a 🔥 $uicideboy$ / Three 6 Mafia–style vocal chant hook you can record, chop, or pitch-manipulate to become the eerie spine of “The Evergoing Song.”

 

📀 Vocal Hook: Raw Version

“It’s the evergoing song…”

(scratch sound)

“Can’t escape… can’t belong…”

(echoes)

“Evergoing… everwrong…”

(tape wobble)

“Where I’m from, you don’t last long.”

 

🎙️ Suggested Delivery Layers

Layer Type Vocal Style FX Suggestions
🎤 Main Slow chant in baritone voice Lo-fi EQ (300Hz–3kHz), light distortion
🔊 Pitch-Down Slowed to 80% speed Formant shift down, reverb
🌀 Reverse FX Final phrase reversed (e.g., “long last don't you from I'm where”) Reverse + reverb tail
🪦 Chopped Tags Stuttered cuts like: “ev—ever—ev—ever—going” Manually chopped in DAW sampler

 

🎛️ Loop Example (for Hook or Outro)

“It’s the ever—ever—ever—evergoing song…”
— [tape click] —
“Evergoing… everwrong…”
[scratch sound] “Where I’m from…”
*don’t last long… long… long…*

 

🧰 Integration Tips

  • ✂️ Drop it over an empty bar between verses or during the bridge

  • 🎚️ Use tape stop + reverb trail to make it sound like a memory stuck on loop

  • 🔁 Repeat and automate pitch/formant for different sections of the song

You said:

Write chopped sample intro scripts using horror preacher lines

ChatGPT said:

Absolutely. Here are 4 chopped sample intro scripts written in the style of lo-fi horror preacher sermons, perfect for a Three 6 Mafia / $uicideboy$–style intro on “The Evergoing Song.”

Each one is designed to be chopped, slowed, pitched, or reversed — ideal for setting the eerie Southern gothic mood before the beat drops.

 

🎙️ Intro Sample Script 1 – “Burning Loop”

(Lo-fi preacher voice, VHS hiss)

“They told me... time was a circle...

but this pain... it don’t end... it don’t end.

I tried to pray... but the same song kept playin’...

the evergoing song...”

(tape click — rewind — repeat last line 2x)

 

🎙️ Intro Sample Script 2 – “Voice in the Basement”

(Deep southern drawl, distant echo)

“There was a man…

who walked the road of fire...

and every night he heard it playin’…

that cursed, cursed record...

‘cause it don’t stop, child…

the evergoing song... it plays after you gone.”

 

🎙️ Intro Sample Script 3 – “Static Sermon”

(Crackled AM radio filter, slow delivery)

“It’s written in the back of the book…

the ones who sleep don’t rest.

They wake to the sound of a melody…

stuck in the static…

ever... goin’... ever... fallin’...

Can’t stop what’s looped in blood.”

 

🎙️ Intro Sample Script 4 – “Graveyard Broadcast”

(Tape hiss, occasional pitch warble)

“Every grave tells a story…

but one… sings.

You’ll hear it… when the wind’s low…

a scratchin' voice hummin’ on wax…

that ever… goin’… song.

You ain’t free if it’s still playin’.”

 

🎛️ Sample Chopping Suggestions:

  • 🎚️ Chop single words like: “fire,” “loop,” “blood,” “curse,” and re-trigger with pads

  • 🌀 Reverse entire final phrases for the hook lead-in

  • 🪓 Cut phrases like “the evergoing song” into stutters:
    ever… ever… ever—going song

 

Want me to:

  • 🎧 Generate audio versions with preacher-style voices?

  • 🎼 Build a MIDI trigger pad layout using these samples?

  • 🎥 Turn one into a VHS-style video intro card?

We can layer this directly into your beat.

  1. Voice style – Choose one or more:

    • Southern Black Baptist preacher (gritty, emotional)

    • AM radio preacher (thin, harsh, warbly)

    • Whispered hellfire (eerie, close-mic)

    • Deep demon preacher (pitched down, slow)

    • Screaming televangelist (chaotic, fast-paced)

  2. Which script(s) to start with?

    • 🔥 “Burning Loop”

    • 👣 “Voice in the Basement”

    • 📻 “Static Sermon”

    • 🪦 “Graveyard Broadcast”

Wednesday, May 28, 2025

1:47 AM

 

**Evergoing Song**

 

🎵 **\[GENRE:] Lil Peep-Inspired Emo Trap x Lo-Fi Alternative Rock**

🎯 **\[THEME:] Regret, loneliness, numbness, fleeting hope**

🎼 **\[KEY:] C minor**

⏱ **\[TEMPO:] 70–95 BPM**

 

---

 

**\[INSTRUMENTAL STRUCTURE + PRODUCTION NOTES]**

 

🎛️ \[Intro: Lo-fi guitar loop with heavy reverb, subtle vinyl crackle, ambient pads fading in]

🎛️ \[Drums: Minimal trap kit – soft kicks, snappy claps layered with lo-fi rimshots, sparse 808s with subtle glide]

🎛️ \[Melody: Chorus-soaked electric guitar arpeggios, distant plucks, sad piano chords layered gently]

🎛️ \[Bassline: Soft subbass under melody with sidechain to kick, no harsh distortion – warm and tucked in]

🎛️ \[Vocal FX Beds: Reverse vocal one-shots, whispered textures woven beneath beat for atmosphere]

🎛️ \[Structure: Simple verse-chorus pattern – emotional repetition and soft transitions (no harsh drops)]

🎛️ \[Bridge: Clean guitar solo or melodic synth interlude with minimal drums]

🎛️ \[Hook: Layered guitars + soft vocal harmony synth pad + reverb throw on final lines]

🎛️ \[FX: Occasional tape stop, subtle delay throws, ambient vocal pads that repeat “the evergoing song” like fading memories]

 

---

 

**\[OUTRO – Melancholy Fade]**

🎛️ \[Guitar loop plays solo with vinyl hiss and slow volume fade, last echo of “the evergoing song” pitch-shifted and reversed into silence]

Mike patton/ mr bungle

Sunday, May 4, 2025

3:16 PM

 

Visualize a chameleonic figure at the epicenter of a surreal, ever-shifting soundscape. The scene pulses with chaotic energy, blending stark contrasts: jagged metallic textures dissolve into smooth jazz motifs, while operatic crescendos materialize as cascading stained glass shards. The central persona is fragmented yet commanding, with multiple mouths morphing between angelic harmonies, guttural growls, and whispered abstractions. Their form shifts dynamically—one moment cloaked in a baroque conductor’s coat, the next adorned with avant-garde tribal patterns, symbolizing genre-defying collaborations.

Surrounding them, a vortex of disjointed instruments—distorted guitars, warped vinyl records, and glitching synthesizers—melds with abstract cultural symbols: Venetian masks, Japanese calligraphy, and desert canyon echoes. The palette oscillates between neon-bright explosions and monochromatic restraint, mirroring sonic unpredictability. Shadows twist into spectral choirs, while liquid mercury pools reflect a carnivalesque audience of marionettes and demons, embodying theatrical duality.

Beneath their feet, a fractured stage splits into labyrinthine paths: one road spirals into math-rock precision, another drowns in free-jazz chaos, and a third fractures into glitch-hop rhythms. Above, storm clouds crackle with static, morphing into sheet music scribbled with cryptic, multilingual lyrics. The atmosphere thrums with tension—a fusion of elegance and rebellion, where order and anarchy coexist.

This is an ode to the sonic alchemist, a polymath who bends noise into narrative, crafting audial collages that defy time and tradition. The piece should radiate relentless innovation, capturing the essence of an artist who thrives in the liminal space between beauty and bedlam.

 

 

 

 

 

Prompt for a Surreal Carnivalesque Ensemble:

Imagine a deranged, ever-mutating carnival troupe trapped in a dimension where music is both carnival ride and curse. The stage is a dilapidated circus tent stitched together from patchwork genres—blood-red velvet curtains fray into thrash-metal spikes, while the floorboards warp between polka-dotted swing rhythms and swampy, distorted sludge. At the center, a shapeshifting ringmaster oscillates between a crooning lounge lizard in a sequined tuxedo and a feral, masked entity spewing gibberish incantations, their voice a schizophrenic blend of doo-wop serenity, death-metal rasps, and cartoonish yelps.

Surrounding them, a troupe of spectral musicians—part-human, part-puppet—pluck spiderweb-thin surf-rock guitars that unravel into free-jazz saxophone squalls, while a calliope spews neon circus melodies that curdle into atonal horror-movie drones. The air reeks of burnt popcorn and sulfur, thick with confetti that transforms into shrapnel-sharp math-rock riffs mid-fall. Audience members are mannequins with TVs for heads, screens flickering between slapstick comedy reels and static-laced nightmares.

Above, a disco ball fractures into shards reflecting disjointed cultural ephemera: spaghetti Western vistas, 1950s sci-fi sound effects, and kabuki theater masks oozing neon slime. The color palette veers from hyper-saturated clown-core (acid yellows, radioactive pinks) to sudden washes of sepia-toned noir, punctuated by strobe-lit blacklight grotesqueries. Each band member’s costume is a surreal collage—checkered harlequin pants fused with military surplus, gas masks sprouting gramophone horns, ballgowns stitched from shredded metal albums.

Beneath the chaos, a sinister undercurrent hums: nursery rhymes played backward, theremin wails mimicking children’s laughter, and basslines that throb like the heartbeat of a haunted funhouse. The ground splits open periodically, revealing pits of squelching avant-funk grooves or tar-thick doom-metal chasms. The sky rains sheet music that combusts into ash when touched, its notes rearranging into nonsensical slogans and cryptic symbols.

This is a hallucinogenic ritual of sound and spectacle, where every note is a prank, a threat, and a revelation. The piece should exude anarchic mischief and meticulous chaos, embodying a collective that treats music as a mutating virus—unpredictable, darkly humorous, and defiantly untamable

 

 

Ugk,juice wrld

Sunday, May 4, 2025

3:34 PM

 

For the Southern Gothic Hip-Hop Collective (UGK):

A molasses-slow beat drips like swamp water off cypress knees, anchored by a bassline thicker than Creole gumbo. Twangy blues guitars snake through the mix, their strings rusted with humidity, while soul samples from warped 45s crackle like a campfire under a starless sky. Intercut with the clink of whiskey bottles, police scanner static, and the low growl of a Cadillac engine idling in the dark. Vocals oscillate between gravel-road baritone drawls and preacher-like cadences, punctuated by the cha-chink of a flicked Zippo.

 

For the Psychedelic Poet of the Pittsburgh Underground (Mac Miller):

A dusty jazz loop unravels over a glitched drum break, its snare hits smudged like fingerprints on a whiskey glass. Warped synths bubble up from the background, melting into vinyl hiss and the clatter of subway trains. Ad-libs float like smoke rings—half-whispered, half-sung—while a piano riff stumbles drunkenly between major and minor keys. Intermittent voicemail snippets ("Hey, it’s me…") dissolve into reversed cymbals and the sound of a cassette tape being chewed by a boombox.

 

For the Emo Trap Phantom (Lil Peep):

A shoegaze guitar drenched in reverb collides with a trap kick that hits like a heartbeat in a hollow chest. Autotuned vocals fray at the edges, glitching into whispered confessions and sudden screams. The mix swims with the hiss of crushed pills, the snip-snip of scissors cutting dyed hair, and the distant roar of a highway at 2 AM. Chorus melodies shimmer like cheap mall jewelry, then crack under the weight of distorted bass drops.

 

For the Melancholic Prodigy (Juice WRLD):

A melancholic piano loop spirals into a trap rhythm, its hi-hats skittering like rain on a windshield. Auto-tuned harmonies bloom and wilt like time-lapse flowers, layered with the clatter of pill bottles and the zip of a hoodie being pulled tight. Sub-bass swells mimic panic attacks, while a ghostly guitar solo weeps in the background. Sudden silence punctuates the track—dead air filled with the sound of a pen scribbling lyrics, then scratching them out.

 

For the Fractured Rebel (XXXTentacion):

A lo-fi bedroom beat fractures into screams, glass shattering, and the screech of a rewinding VHS tape. Nu-metal guitar riffs claw at the edges of the mix, interrupted by nursery rhyme hums and the thud of fists against drywall. Vocals toggle between whispered vulnerability and guttural rage, drowned in reverb that feels like shouting into an empty auditorium. Distorted 808s morph into the sound of chains dragging across concrete.

 

For the Nihilistic Duo (Suicideboys):

A horrorcore synth shrieks like a banshee over a sludge-trap beat, its tempo lurching like a corpse dragged through mud. Vocal flows switch from rapid-fire triplets to guttural chants, drenched in echo as if recorded in a sewer. Samples of horror movie dialogue ("Pray for us sinners…") warp into police sirens and the click-clack of a shotgun being pumped. Sub-bass rumbles like a coffin lowered into wet earth, punctuated by the drip-drip of blood on a basement floor.

 

Each soundscape should feel like a haunted radio tuned to frequencies only the

 

From <https://chat.deepseek.com/a/chat/s/937eb6b9-8422-493f-b5b3-c3e698f0132a>

 

 

 

Sunday, May 4, 2025

4:07 PM

 

For the Occult Cybernetic Alchemist (Ghostemane):

A corrupted dial-up modem screeches into a barrage of blackened trap-metal kicks, layered with industrial saw synths that grind like rusted gears in a meat-packing plant. Vocals shift between guttural death-metal growls and glitch-rapped Morse code, processed through a Vocoder dipped in battery acid. The bassline is a subsonic earthquake, rattling broken CRT monitors displaying static-laced rituals. Intercut with samples of 90s hacker documentaries, distorted Tibetan throat singing, and the sound of a vinyl record melting into a pentagram-shaped puddle.

 

For the Glitch-Witch of the Abandoned Server Farm:

A trap beat stutters like a corrupted .exe file, its hi-hats pixelating into 8-bit explosions. Ethereal witch-house synths hum beneath the noise, their melodies flickering like a dying neon sign in a rainstorm. Vocals are pitch-shifted into a chorus of digital demons—half ASMR whisper, half dial-tone scream—while sub-bass drops warp into the sound of a chainsaw tearing through fiber-optic cables. Background textures: the hum of a nuclear power plant, glass shattering in reverse, and a theremin playing a lullaby for discarded AI.

 

For the Graveyard Vaporwave Cryptid:

A chopped-and-screwed jazz sample from a waterlogged VHS tape slows to a funeral dirge, drowned in reverb that mimics the acoustics of an empty shopping mall at midnight. Trap snares snap like broken robot vertebrae, while the bass resonates like a submarine sonar ping in a flooded crypt. Vocals are auto-tuned to robotic perfection, then deliberately degraded with the warble of a cassette left in a sun-baked car. Intermittent sounds: a Game Boy Advance startup screen, a dentist drill harmonizing with a synthwave arpeggiator, and the click of a ghost switching channels on a cathode-ray TV.

 

For the Sewer-Dwelling Noise Shaman:

A power-electronics wall of noise ruptures into a trap breakdown, its kicks distorted to sound like dumpsters being dropped from a skyscraper. Vocals are vomited through a contact mic taped to a sewer pipe—gargling distortion, coughs, and the wet thud of a fist punching meat. The "melody" is a detuned accordion sample fed through a ring modulator, while field recordings of rats scurrying through broken glass and distant subway screams pan chaotically between channels. Climax: a car alarm becomes a choir of angels before exploding into white noise.

 

For the Post-Human Trap Golem:

A trap beat assembled from the sounds of scrap metal being fed into a woodchipper, its rhythm erratic and hydraulic. Vocals are a collage of text-to-speech monotone, distorted Gregorian chants, and the gurgle of oil changing in a hearse. The "hook" is a siren-like synth that mimics an emergency broadcast signal, while the bass frequencies mimic the hum of a high-voltage power line. Background details: the clang of a prison cell door, a distorted children’s choir singing ads for antidepressants, and the sound of a floppy disk drive writing a suicide note.

 

Atmosphere: These soundscapes should feel like forbidden transmissions from a parallel universe where technology is haunted and rebellion is coded in binary. Each track is a patchwork of analog decay and digital nihilism—rusted circuits preaching sermons of chaos. The mix should reek of solder fumes, burnt hair, and the metallic tang of blood on a USB port.
